## Best Practices

    - Keep text concise and engaging
    - Use mentions sparingly to avoid spam
    - Add relevant hashtags for discoverability
    - Include context for better user engagement

    - Validate text length before submission
    - Handle network errors gracefully
    - Provide user feedback during creation
    - Cache drafts for better user experience

    - Show character count indicators
    - Provide auto-complete for mentions
    - Suggest relevant hashtags
    - Allow draft saving and editing

## Common Use Cases

- **Status Updates**: Share personal thoughts and experiences
- **Community Discussions**: Start conversations in community feeds
- **Announcements**: Broadcast important information
- **Questions**: Ask for advice or opinions from followers
- **Storytelling**: Share experiences and narratives
- **Links Sharing**: Share URLs with context and commentary

### [Image Posts](https://docs.socialplus.com/social-plus-sdk/social/posts/create-post/image-post)

> Create posts with single or multiple images, captions, and rich media support

Share visual content with support for multiple images, captions, and metadata. Image posts support up to 10 images per post with rich text descriptions.

## Overview

Image posts combine visual content with descriptive text:

- **Multiple Images**: Up to 10 images per post
- **Rich Captions**: Text descriptions with mentions and hashtags
- **File Size Limit**: Up to 1GB per image
- **Parent-Child Structure**: Each image becomes a child post
- **Interactive Features**: Users can react to individual images

  Images must be uploaded first before creating the post. See [Image
  Handling](/social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/image-handling)
  for upload instructions.

## Create Image Post

```swift
import AmitySDK

class ImagePostManager {
    private let postRepository: AmityPostRepository
    private let fileRepository: AmityFileRepository

    init(client: AmityClient) {
        self.postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client: client)
        self.fileRepository = AmityFileRepository(client: client)
    }

    func createImagePost(
        title: String? = nil,
        text: String,
        images: [UIImage],
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = nil,
        tags: [String] = [],
        metadata: [String: Any]? = nil
    ) {
        // First upload images
        uploadImages(images) { [weak self] uploadedImages in
            self?.createPostWithUploadedImages(
                title: title,
                text: text,
                uploadedImages: uploadedImages,
                targetType: targetType,
                targetId: targetId,
                tags: tags,
                metadata: metadata
            )
        }
    }

    private func uploadImages(
        _ images: [UIImage],
        completion: @escaping ([AmityImageData]) -> Void
    ) {
        var uploadedImages: [AmityImageData] = []
        let group = DispatchGroup()

        for image in images {
            group.enter()
            fileRepository.uploadImage(image) { result in
                defer { group.leave() }

                switch result {
                case .success(let imageData):
                    uploadedImages.append(imageData)
                case .failure(let error):
                    print("Failed to upload image: \(error)")
                }
            }
        }

        group.notify(queue: .main) {
            completion(uploadedImages)
        }
    }

    private func createPostWithUploadedImages(
        title: String?,
        text: String,
        uploadedImages: [AmityImageData],
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String?,
        tags: [String],
        metadata: [String: Any]?
    ) {
        let builder = AmityImagePostBuilder()
            .setText(text)
            .setTargetType(targetType)
            .setImages(uploadedImages)
            .setTags(tags)

        if let title = title {
            builder.setTitle(title)
        }

        if let targetId = targetId {
            builder.setTargetId(targetId)
        }

        if let metadata = metadata {
            builder.setMetadata(metadata)
        }

        postRepository.createPost(builder) { [weak self] result in
            DispatchQueue.main.async {
                switch result {
                case .success(let post):
                    print("Image post created: \(post.postId)")
                    self?.handlePostCreated(post)

                case .failure(let error):
                    print("Failed to create image post: \(error)")
                    self?.handlePostError(error)
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Create single image post
    func createSingleImagePost(
        text: String,
        image: UIImage,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = nil
    ) {
        createImagePost(
            text: text,
            images: [image],
            targetType: targetType,
            targetId: targetId
        )
    }

    private func handlePostCreated(_ post: AmityPost) {
        // Handle success
    }

    private func handlePostError(_ error: AmityError) {
        // Handle error
    }
}
```


```kotlin
import com.amity.socialcloud.sdk.api.social.AmitySocialClient
import com.amity.socialcloud.sdk.model.social.post.AmityPost

class ImagePostManager(private val client: AmitySocialClient) {

    fun createImagePost(
        title: String? = null,
        text: String,
        imageFiles: List<File>,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null,
        tags: List<String> = emptyList(),
        metadata: Map<String, Any>? = null
    ) {
        // First upload images
        uploadImages(imageFiles) { uploadedImages ->
            createPostWithUploadedImages(
                title, text, uploadedImages, targetType, targetId, tags, metadata
            )
        }
    }

    private fun uploadImages(
        imageFiles: List<File>,
        onComplete: (List<AmityUploadResult>) -> Unit
    ) {
        val uploads = mutableListOf<AmityUploadResult>()
        var completed = 0

        imageFiles.forEach { file ->
            client.file()
                .uploadImage(file)
                .subscribe({ uploadResult ->
                    uploads.add(uploadResult)
                    completed++

                    if (completed == imageFiles.size) {
                        onComplete(uploads)
                    }
                }, { error ->
                    println("Failed to upload image: ${error.message}")
                    completed++

                    if (completed == imageFiles.size) {
                        onComplete(uploads)
                    }
                })
        }
    }

    private fun createPostWithUploadedImages(
        title: String?,
        text: String,
        uploadedImages: List<AmityUploadResult>,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String?,
        tags: List<String>,
        metadata: Map<String, Any>?
    ) {
        val builder = AmityImagePostBuilder()
            .setText(text)
            .setTargetType(targetType)
            .setImages(uploadedImages)
            .setTags(tags)

        title?.let { builder.setTitle(it) }
        targetId?.let { builder.setTargetId(it) }
        metadata?.let { builder.setMetadata(it) }

        client.post()
            .createPost(builder)
            .subscribeOn(Schedulers.io())
            .observeOn(AndroidSchedulers.mainThread())
            .subscribe(
                { post ->
                    println("Image post created: ${post.postId}")
                    handlePostCreated(post)
                },
                { error ->
                    println("Failed to create image post: ${error.message}")
                    handlePostError(error)
                }
            )
    }

    // Create single image post
    fun createSingleImagePost(
        text: String,
        imageFile: File,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null
    ) {
        createImagePost(
            text = text,
            imageFiles = listOf(imageFile),
            targetType = targetType,
            targetId = targetId
        )
    }

    // Create image post with compression
    fun createCompressedImagePost(
        text: String,
        imageFiles: List<File>,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null,
        quality: Int = 80
    ) {
        val compressedFiles = compressImages(imageFiles, quality)
        createImagePost(text, compressedFiles, targetType, targetId)
    }

    private fun compressImages(files: List<File>, quality: Int): List<File> {
        // Implement image compression logic
        return files // Placeholder
    }

    private fun handlePostCreated(post: AmityPost) {
        // Handle success
    }

    private fun handlePostError(error: Throwable) {
        // Handle error
    }
}
```


```typescript
import { PostRepository, PostContentType } from '@amityco/ts-sdk';

async function createImagePost(): Promise<Amity.Post> {
    const newPost = {
        data: {
            title: 'Greeting!',
            text: 'hello!',
        },
        attachments: [
            { type: PostContentType.IMAGE, fileId: 'imageFileId1' },
            { type: PostContentType.IMAGE, fileId: 'imageFileId2' },
        ],
        targetType: 'user',
        targetId: 'userId1',
    };

    const { data: post } = await PostRepository.createPost(newPost);

    return post;
}
```

```dart
import 'package:amity_sdk/amity_sdk.dart';
import 'dart:io';

class ImagePostManager {
    final AmitySocialClient _socialClient;

    ImagePostManager(this._socialClient);

    Future<AmityPost> createImagePost({
        required String text,
        required List<File> imageFiles,
        required AmityPostTargetType targetType,
        String? targetId,
        List<String> tags = const [],
        Map<String, dynamic>? metadata,
    }) async {
        try {
            // First upload images
            final uploadedImages = await _uploadImages(imageFiles);

            // Create post with uploaded images
            final builder = AmityImagePostBuilder()
                ..setText(text)
                ..setTargetType(targetType)
                ..setImages(uploadedImages)
                ..setTags(tags);

            if (targetId != null) {
                builder.setTargetId(targetId);
            }

            if (metadata != null) {
                builder.setMetadata(metadata);
            }

            final post = await _socialClient
                .newPostRepository()
                .createPost(builder);

            print('Image post created: ${post.postId}');
            _handlePostCreated(post);

            return post;

        } catch (error) {
            print('Failed to create image post: $error');
            _handlePostError(error);
            rethrow;
        }
    }

    Future<List<AmityUploadResult>> _uploadImages(List<File> imageFiles) async {
        final List<Future<AmityUploadResult>> uploadFutures = imageFiles
            .map((file) => _socialClient.newFileRepository().uploadImage(file))
            .toList();

        try {
            final results = await Future.wait(uploadFutures);
            return results.where((result) => result.fileId != null).toList();
        } catch (error) {
            print('Some images failed to upload: $error');
            rethrow;
        }
    }

    // Create single image post
    Future<AmityPost> createSingleImagePost(
        String text,
        File imageFile, {
        AmityPostTargetType targetType = AmityPostTargetType.USER,
        String? targetId,
    }) async {
        return createImagePost(
            text: text,
            imageFiles: [imageFile],
            targetType: targetType,
            targetId: targetId,
        );
    }

    // Create image post with compression
    Future<AmityPost> createCompressedImagePost({
        required String text,
        required List<File> imageFiles,
        required AmityPostTargetType targetType,
        String? targetId,
        int quality = 80,
    }) async {
        // Compress images before upload
        final compressedFiles = await _compressImages(imageFiles, quality);

        return createImagePost(
            text: text,
            imageFiles: compressedFiles,
            targetType: targetType,
            targetId: targetId,
        );
    }

    Future<List<File>> _compressImages(List<File> files, int quality) async {
        // Implement image compression logic
        // This is a placeholder - use image compression package
        return files;
    }

    // Create image post with progress callback
    Future<AmityPost> createImagePostWithProgress({
        required String text,
        required List<File> imageFiles,
        required AmityPostTargetType targetType,
        String? targetId,
        Function(double progress)? onProgress,
    }) async {
        try {
            int uploadedCount = 0;
            final uploadedImages = <AmityUploadResult>[];

            for (final file in imageFiles) {
                final result = await _socialClient
                    .newFileRepository()
                    .uploadImage(file);

                uploadedImages.add(result);
                uploadedCount++;

                onProgress?.call(uploadedCount / imageFiles.length);
            }

            final builder = AmityImagePostBuilder()
                ..setText(text)
                ..setTargetType(targetType)
                ..setImages(uploadedImages);

            if (targetId != null) {
                builder.setTargetId(targetId);
            }

            return await _socialClient
                .newPostRepository()
                .createPost(builder);

        } catch (error) {
            print('Failed to create image post with progress: $error');
            rethrow;
        }
    }

    void _handlePostCreated(AmityPost post) {
        print('Image post created successfully: ${post.postId}');
    }

    void _handlePostError(dynamic error) {
        print('Image post creation failed: $error');
    }
}
```

## Parameters

| Parameter           | Type            | Required | Description                          |
| ------------------- | --------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `title`             | String          | ❌       | Post title (max 150 characters)      |
| `text`              | String          | ✅       | Caption text (max 20,000 characters) |
| `images/imageFiles` | Array           | ✅       | Image files or uploaded image data   |
| `targetType`        | Enum            | ✅       | Target type (`user` or `community`)  |
| `targetId`          | String          | ❌       | Target ID (null for own feed)        |
| `tags`              | Array\<String\> | ❌       | Tags for categorization              |
| `metadata`          | Object          | ❌       | Custom metadata                      |

## Image Requirements

| Requirement      | Limit                | Notes                                |
| ---------------- | -------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| **File Size**    | Up to 1GB per image  | Platform may have lower limits       |
| **Image Count**  | Maximum 10 images    | Per single post                      |
| **File Formats** | JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP | Platform dependent                   |
| **Resolution**   | No fixed limit       | Higher resolution = larger file size |

## Advanced Features

### Image Optimization

```swift
// Resize image before upload
func resizeImage(_ image: UIImage, targetSize: CGSize) -> UIImage {
    let renderer = UIGraphicsImageRenderer(size: targetSize)
    return renderer.image { _ in
        image.draw(in: CGRect(origin: .zero, size: targetSize))
    }
}

// Create post with optimized images
let optimizedImages = originalImages.map { image in
    resizeImage(image, targetSize: CGSize(width: 1080, height: 1080))
}

imagePostManager.createImagePost(
    text: "Beautiful sunset!",
    images: optimizedImages,
    targetType: .community,
    targetId: "photography"
)
```

```kotlin
// Compress image before upload
fun compressImage(file: File, quality: Int = 80): File {
    val bitmap = BitmapFactory.decodeFile(file.absolutePath)
    val outputStream = ByteArrayOutputStream()
    bitmap.compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.JPEG, quality, outputStream)

    val compressedFile = File(file.parent, "compressed_${file.name}")
    FileOutputStream(compressedFile).use { fos ->
        fos.write(outputStream.toByteArray())
    }

    return compressedFile
}

// Create post with compressed images
val compressedImages = imageFiles.map { compressImage(it, 80) }

imagePostManager.createImagePost(
    text = "Beautiful sunset!",
    imageFiles = compressedImages,
    targetType = AmityPostTargetType.COMMUNITY,
    targetId = "photography"
)
```

```typescript
// Resize image before upload
function resizeImage(file: File, maxWidth: number, maxHeight: number): Promise<File> {
    return new Promise((resolve) => {
        const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
        const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d')!;
        const img = new Image();

        img.onload = () => {
            const { width, height } = calculateNewDimensions(
                img.width, img.height, maxWidth, maxHeight
            );

            canvas.width = width;
            canvas.height = height;

            ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, width, height);

            canvas.toBlob((blob) => {
                const newFile = new File([blob!], file.name, { type: 'image/jpeg' });
                resolve(newFile);
            }, 'image/jpeg', 0.8);
        };

        img.src = URL.createObjectURL(file);
    });
}

// Create post with optimized images
const optimizedImages = await Promise.all(
    imageFiles.map(file => resizeImage(file, 1080, 1080))
);

await imagePostManager.createImagePost({
    text: "Beautiful sunset!",
    imageFiles: optimizedImages,
    targetType: 'community',
    targetId: 'photography'
});
```

```dart
import 'package:image/image.dart' as img;

// Compress image before upload
Future<File> compressImage(File file, {int quality = 80}) async {
    final bytes = await file.readAsBytes();
    final image = img.decodeImage(bytes);

    if (image == null) return file;

    final compressedBytes = img.encodeJpg(image, quality: quality);

    final compressedFile = File('${file.path}_compressed.jpg');
    await compressedFile.writeAsBytes(compressedBytes);

    return compressedFile;
}

// Create post with compressed images
final compressedImages = <File>[];
for (final file in imageFiles) {
    final compressed = await compressImage(file, quality: 80);
    compressedImages.add(compressed);
}

await imagePostManager.createImagePost(
    text: "Beautiful sunset!",
    imageFiles: compressedImages,
    targetType: AmityPostTargetType.COMMUNITY,
    targetId: 'photography',
);
```

## Best Practices

    - Compress images before upload to reduce file size
    - Resize images to appropriate dimensions (e.g., 1080x1080)
    - Use appropriate file formats (JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics)
    - Implement progress indicators for large uploads

    - Show image previews before posting
    - Allow users to reorder images
    - Provide crop and edit functionality
    - Handle upload failures gracefully with retry options

    - Upload images in parallel when possible
    - Cache uploaded images to avoid re-uploads
    - Implement lazy loading for image previews
    - Use appropriate image loading libraries

## Common Use Cases

- **Photo Sharing**: Share personal photos and memories
- **Product Showcases**: Display products with multiple angles
- **Event Documentation**: Share photos from events and gatherings
- **Before/After Comparisons**: Show transformations or progress
- **Step-by-Step Guides**: Visual tutorials and instructions
- **Gallery Posts**: Curated collections of related images

### [Video Posts](https://docs.socialplus.com/social-plus-sdk/social/posts/create-post/video-post)

> Create posts with video content, thumbnails, and rich media support

Share video content with support for multiple videos, captions, and metadata. Video posts support up to 10 videos per post with automatic thumbnail generation and rich text descriptions.

## Overview

Video posts combine visual storytelling with descriptive text:

- **Multiple Videos**: Up to 10 videos per post
- **Rich Captions**: Text descriptions with mentions and hashtags
- **File Size Limit**: Up to 2GB per video
- **Duration Limit**: Maximum 2 hours per video
- **Format Support**: Wide range of video formats
- **Parent-Child Structure**: Each video becomes a child post

  Videos must be uploaded first before creating the post. See [Video
  Handling](/social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/video-handling)
  for upload instructions.

## Video Requirements

| Requirement | Limit | Notes |
|-------------|-------|-------|
| **File Size** | Up to 2GB per video | Platform may have lower limits |
| **Duration** | Maximum 2 hours | Per individual video |
| **Video Count** | Maximum 10 videos | Per single post |
| **File Formats** | `mp4`, `mov`, `avi`, `mkv`, `webm`, `flv`, `3gp`, `wmv`, `vob`, `ogv`, `3g2`, `f4v`, `m4v` | Platform dependent |


## Create Video Post

```swift
import AmitySDK

class VideoPostManager {
    private let postRepository: AmityPostRepository
    private let fileRepository: AmityFileRepository

    init(client: AmityClient) {
        self.postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client: client)
        self.fileRepository = AmityFileRepository(client: client)
    }

    func createVideoPost(
        title: String? = nil,
        text: String,
        videoURLs: [URL],
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = nil,
        tags: [String] = [],
        metadata: [String: Any]? = nil
    ) {
        // First upload videos
        uploadVideos(videoURLs) { [weak self] uploadedVideos in
            self?.createPostWithUploadedVideos(
                title: title,
                text: text,
                uploadedVideos: uploadedVideos,
                targetType: targetType,
                targetId: targetId,
                tags: tags,
                metadata: metadata
            )
        }
    }

    private func uploadVideos(
        _ videoURLs: [URL],
        completion: @escaping ([AmityVideoData]) -> Void
    ) {
        var uploadedVideos: [AmityVideoData] = []
        let group = DispatchGroup()

        for videoURL in videoURLs {
            group.enter()
            fileRepository.uploadVideo(videoURL) { result in
                defer { group.leave() }

                switch result {
                case .success(let videoData):
                    uploadedVideos.append(videoData)
                case .failure(let error):
                    print("Failed to upload video: \(error)")
                }
            }
        }

        group.notify(queue: .main) {
            completion(uploadedVideos)
        }
    }

    private func createPostWithUploadedVideos(
        title: String?,
        text: String,
        uploadedVideos: [AmityVideoData],
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String?,
        tags: [String],
        metadata: [String: Any]?
    ) {
        let builder = AmityVideoPostBuilder()
            .setText(text)
            .setTargetType(targetType)
            .setVideos(uploadedVideos)
            .setTags(tags)

        if let title = title {
            builder.setTitle(title)
        }

        if let targetId = targetId {
            builder.setTargetId(targetId)
        }

        if let metadata = metadata {
            builder.setMetadata(metadata)
        }

        postRepository.createPost(builder) { [weak self] result in
            DispatchQueue.main.async {
                switch result {
                case .success(let post):
                    print("Video post created: \(post.postId)")
                    self?.handlePostCreated(post)

                case .failure(let error):
                    print("Failed to create video post: \(error)")
                    self?.handlePostError(error)
                }
            }
        }
    }

    // Create single video post
    func createSingleVideoPost(
        text: String,
        videoURL: URL,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = nil
    ) {
        createVideoPost(
            text: text,
            videoURLs: [videoURL],
            targetType: targetType,
            targetId: targetId
        )
    }

    // Create video post with thumbnail
    func createVideoPostWithThumbnail(
        text: String,
        videoURL: URL,
        thumbnailImage: UIImage,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = nil
    ) {
        // Upload video with custom thumbnail
        fileRepository.uploadVideo(
            videoURL,
            thumbnail: thumbnailImage
        ) { [weak self] result in
            switch result {
            case .success(let videoData):
                self?.createPostWithUploadedVideos(
                    text: text,
                    uploadedVideos: [videoData],
                    targetType: targetType,
                    targetId: targetId,
                    tags: [],
                    metadata: nil
                )
            case .failure(let error):
                print("Failed to upload video: \(error)")
                self?.handlePostError(error)
            }
        }
    }

    private func handlePostCreated(_ post: AmityPost) {
        // Handle success
    }

    private func handlePostError(_ error: AmityError) {
        // Handle error
    }
}
```


```kotlin
import com.amity.socialcloud.sdk.api.social.AmitySocialClient
import com.amity.socialcloud.sdk.model.social.post.AmityPost

class VideoPostManager(private val client: AmitySocialClient) {

    fun createVideoPost(
        title: String? = null,
        text: String,
        videoFiles: List<File>,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null,
        tags: List<String> = emptyList(),
        metadata: Map<String, Any>? = null
    ) {
        // First upload videos
        uploadVideos(videoFiles) { uploadedVideos ->
            createPostWithUploadedVideos(
                title, text, uploadedVideos, targetType, targetId, tags, metadata
            )
        }
    }

    private fun uploadVideos(
        videoFiles: List<File>,
        onComplete: (List<AmityUploadResult>) -> Unit
    ) {
        val uploads = mutableListOf<AmityUploadResult>()
        var completed = 0

        videoFiles.forEach { file ->
            client.file()
                .uploadVideo(file)
                .subscribe({ uploadResult ->
                    uploads.add(uploadResult)
                    completed++

                    if (completed == videoFiles.size) {
                        onComplete(uploads)
                    }
                }, { error ->
                    println("Failed to upload video: ${error.message}")
                    completed++

                    if (completed == videoFiles.size) {
                        onComplete(uploads)
                    }
                })
        }
    }

    private fun createPostWithUploadedVideos(
        title: String?,
        text: String,
        uploadedVideos: List<AmityUploadResult>,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String?,
        tags: List<String>,
        metadata: Map<String, Any>?
    ) {
        val builder = AmityVideoPostBuilder()
            .setText(text)
            .setTargetType(targetType)
            .setVideos(uploadedVideos)
            .setTags(tags)

        title?.let { builder.setTitle(it) }
        targetId?.let { builder.setTargetId(it) }
        metadata?.let { builder.setMetadata(it) }

        client.post()
            .createPost(builder)
            .subscribeOn(Schedulers.io())
            .observeOn(AndroidSchedulers.mainThread())
            .subscribe(
                { post ->
                    println("Video post created: ${post.postId}")
                    handlePostCreated(post)
                },
                { error ->
                    println("Failed to create video post: ${error.message}")
                    handlePostError(error)
                }
            )
    }

    // Create single video post
    fun createSingleVideoPost(
        text: String,
        videoFile: File,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null
    ) {
        createVideoPost(
            text = text,
            videoFiles = listOf(videoFile),
            targetType = targetType,
            targetId = targetId
        )
    }

    // Create video post with compression
    fun createCompressedVideoPost(
        text: String,
        videoFile: File,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null,
        quality: VideoQuality = VideoQuality.MEDIUM
    ) {
        val compressedFile = compressVideo(videoFile, quality)
        createSingleVideoPost(text, compressedFile, targetType, targetId)
    }

    // Create video post with progress tracking
    fun createVideoPostWithProgress(
        text: String,
        videoFiles: List<File>,
        targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
        targetId: String? = null,
        onProgress: (Int, Int) -> Unit // (current, total)
    ) {
        var uploadedCount = 0
        val uploadedVideos = mutableListOf<AmityUploadResult>()

        videoFiles.forEachIndexed { index, file ->
            client.file()
                .uploadVideo(file)
                .subscribe({ uploadResult ->
                    uploadedVideos.add(uploadResult)
                    uploadedCount++
                    onProgress(uploadedCount, videoFiles.size)

                    if (uploadedCount == videoFiles.size) {
                        createPostWithUploadedVideos(
                            text, uploadedVideos, targetType, targetId, emptyList(), null
                        )
                    }
                }, { error ->
                    println("Failed to upload video ${index + 1}: ${error.message}")
                })
        }
    }

    private fun compressVideo(file: File, quality: VideoQuality): File {
        // Implement video compression logic
        return file // Placeholder
    }

    private fun handlePostCreated(post: AmityPost) {
        // Handle success
    }

    private fun handlePostError(error: Throwable) {
        // Handle error
    }
}

enum class VideoQuality { LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH }
```


```typescript
import { PostRepository, PostContentType } from '@amityco/ts-sdk';

async function createVideoPost(): Promise<Amity.Post> {
    const newPost = {
        data: {
            title: 'Greeting',
            text: 'hello!',
        },
        attachments: [
            { type: PostContentType.VIDEO, fileId: 'videoFileId1' },
            { type: PostContentType.VIDEO, fileId: 'videoFileId' },
        ],
        targetType: 'user',
        targetId: 'userId1',
    };

    const { data: post } = await PostRepository.createPost(newPost);

    return post;
}
```

```dart
import 'package:amity_sdk/amity_sdk.dart';
import 'dart:io';

class VideoPostManager {
    final AmitySocialClient _socialClient;

    VideoPostManager(this._socialClient);

    Future<AmityPost> createVideoPost({
        String? title,
        required String text,
        required List<File> videoFiles,
        required AmityPostTargetType targetType,
        String? targetId,
        List<String> tags = const [],
        Map<String, dynamic>? metadata,
    }) async {
        try {
            // First upload videos
            final uploadedVideos = await _uploadVideos(videoFiles);

            // Create post with uploaded videos
            final builder = AmityVideoPostBuilder()
                ..setText(text)
                ..setTargetType(targetType)
                ..setVideos(uploadedVideos)
                ..setTags(tags);

            if (title != null) {
                builder.setTitle(title);
            }

            if (targetId != null) {
                builder.setTargetId(targetId);
            }

            if (metadata != null) {
                builder.setMetadata(metadata);
            }

            final post = await _socialClient
                .newPostRepository()
                .createPost(builder);

            print('Video post created: ${post.postId}');
            _handlePostCreated(post);

            return post;

        } catch (error) {
            print('Failed to create video post: $error');
            _handlePostError(error);
            rethrow;
        }
    }

    Future<List<AmityUploadResult>> _uploadVideos(List<File> videoFiles) async {
        final List<Future<AmityUploadResult>> uploadFutures = videoFiles
            .map((file) => _socialClient.newFileRepository().uploadVideo(file))
            .toList();

        try {
            final results = await Future.wait(uploadFutures);
            return results.where((result) => result.fileId != null).toList();
        } catch (error) {
            print('Some videos failed to upload: $error');
            rethrow;
        }
    }

    // Create single video post
    Future<AmityPost> createSingleVideoPost(
        String text,
        File videoFile, {
        AmityPostTargetType targetType = AmityPostTargetType.USER,
        String? targetId,
    }) async {
        return createVideoPost(
            text: text,
            videoFiles: [videoFile],
            targetType: targetType,
            targetId: targetId,
        );
    }

    // Create video post with compression
    Future<AmityPost> createCompressedVideoPost({
        required String text,
        required File videoFile,
        required AmityPostTargetType targetType,
        String? targetId,
        VideoQuality quality = VideoQuality.medium,
    }) async {
        // Compress video before upload
        final compressedFile = await _compressVideo(videoFile, quality);

        return createSingleVideoPost(
            text,
            compressedFile,
            targetType: targetType,
            targetId: targetId,
        );
    }

    // Create video post with progress callback
    Future<AmityPost> createVideoPostWithProgress({
        required String text,
        required List<File> videoFiles,
        required AmityPostTargetType targetType,
        String? targetId,
        Function(double progress)? onProgress,
    }) async {
        try {
            int uploadedCount = 0;
            final uploadedVideos = <AmityUploadResult>[];

            for (final file in videoFiles) {
                final result = await _socialClient
                    .newFileRepository()
                    .uploadVideo(file);

                uploadedVideos.add(result);
                uploadedCount++;

                onProgress?.call(uploadedCount / videoFiles.length);
            }

            final builder = AmityVideoPostBuilder()
                ..setText(text)
                ..setTargetType(targetType)
                ..setVideos(uploadedVideos);

            if (targetId != null) {
                builder.setTargetId(targetId);
            }

            return await _socialClient
                .newPostRepository()
                .createPost(builder);

        } catch (error) {
            print('Failed to create video post with progress: $error');
            rethrow;
        }
    }

    Future<File> _compressVideo(File file, VideoQuality quality) async {
        // Implement video compression logic
        // This is a placeholder - use video compression package
        print('Compressing video with quality: $quality');
        return file;
    }

    // Create thumbnail from video
    Future<File?> createThumbnail(File videoFile) async {
        try {
            // Implement thumbnail generation
            // This would typically use a video processing package
            print('Creating thumbnail for video: ${videoFile.path}');
            return null; // Placeholder
        } catch (error) {
            print('Failed to create thumbnail: $error');
            return null;
        }
    }

    void _handlePostCreated(AmityPost post) {
        print('Video post created successfully: ${post.postId}');
    }

    void _handlePostError(dynamic error) {
        print('Video post creation failed: $error');
    }
}

enum VideoQuality { low, medium, high }
```


## Parameters

| Parameter           | Type            | Required | Description                          |
| ------------------- | --------------- | -------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `title`             | String          | ❌       | Post title (max 150 characters)      |
| `text`              | String          | ✅       | Caption text (max 20,000 characters) |
| `videos/videoFiles` | Array           | ✅       | Video files or uploaded video data   |
| `targetType`        | Enum            | ✅       | Target type (`user` or `community`)  |
| `targetId`          | String          | ❌       | Target ID (null for own feed)        |
| `tags`              | Array\<String\> | ❌       | Tags for categorization              |
| `metadata`          | Object          | ❌       | Custom metadata                      |

## Advanced Features

### Video Compression and Optimization

```swift
// Compress video before upload
func compressVideo(_ inputURL: URL, quality: String) -> URL? {
    let asset = AVAsset(url: inputURL)
    let exportSession = AVAssetExportSession(asset: asset, presetName: quality)

    let outputURL = URL(fileURLWithPath: NSTemporaryDirectory())
        .appendingPathComponent("compressed_video.mp4")

    exportSession?.outputURL = outputURL
    exportSession?.outputFileType = .mp4

    let semaphore = DispatchSemaphore(value: 0)
    var result: URL?

    exportSession?.exportAsynchronously {
        if exportSession?.status == .completed {
            result = outputURL
        }
        semaphore.signal()
    }

    semaphore.wait()
    return result
}

// Create video post with compression
let compressedURL = compressVideo(originalURL, quality: AVAssetExportPresetMediumQuality)
if let compressedURL = compressedURL {
    videoPostManager.createSingleVideoPost(
        text: "Check out this video!",
        videoURL: compressedURL,
        targetType: .community,
        targetId: "video_community"
    )
}
```

```kotlin
// Video compression with MediaMetadataRetriever
fun getVideoInfo(file: File): VideoInfo {
    val retriever = MediaMetadataRetriever()
    return try {
        retriever.setDataSource(file.absolutePath)

        val duration = retriever.extractMetadata(MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_DURATION)?.toLong() ?: 0
        val width = retriever.extractMetadata(MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_VIDEO_WIDTH)?.toInt() ?: 0
        val height = retriever.extractMetadata(MediaMetadataRetriever.METADATA_KEY_VIDEO_HEIGHT)?.toInt() ?: 0
        val size = file.length()

        VideoInfo(duration, width, height, size)
    } finally {
        retriever.release()
    }
}

// Create video post with validation
fun createValidatedVideoPost(
    text: String,
    videoFile: File,
    targetType: AmityPostTargetType,
    targetId: String? = null
) {
    val videoInfo = getVideoInfo(videoFile)

    // Validate video constraints
    when {
        videoInfo.size > 2_147_483_648L -> { // 2GB limit
            println("Video file too large: ${videoInfo.size / (1024 * 1024)}MB")
            return
        }
        videoInfo.duration > 7_200_000L -> { // 2 hours limit
            println("Video too long: ${videoInfo.duration / 60000} minutes")
            return
        }
        else -> {
            createSingleVideoPost(text, videoFile, targetType, targetId)
        }
    }
}

data class VideoInfo(
    val duration: Long, // milliseconds
    val width: Int,
    val height: Int,
    val size: Long // bytes
)
```

```typescript
// Video validation and compression
interface VideoInfo {
    duration: number;
    width: number;
    height: number;
    size: number;
}

async getVideoInfo(file: File): Promise<VideoInfo> {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        const video = document.createElement('video');

        video.onloadedmetadata = () => {
            resolve({
                duration: video.duration * 1000, // Convert to milliseconds
                width: video.videoWidth,
                height: video.videoHeight,
                size: file.size
            });
        };

        video.onerror = reject;
        video.src = URL.createObjectURL(file);
    });
}

// Create video post with validation
async createValidatedVideoPost(
    text: string,
    videoFile: File,
    targetType: PostTargetType = 'user',
    targetId?: string
): Promise<Post> {
    const videoInfo = await this.getVideoInfo(videoFile);

    // Validate video constraints
    const MAX_SIZE = 2 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024; // 2GB
    const MAX_DURATION = 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 2 hours in milliseconds

    if (videoInfo.size > MAX_SIZE) {
        throw new Error(`Video file too large: ${Math.round(videoInfo.size / (1024 * 1024))}MB`);
    }

    if (videoInfo.duration > MAX_DURATION) {
        throw new Error(`Video too long: ${Math.round(videoInfo.duration / 60000)} minutes`);
    }

    return this.createSingleVideoPost(text, videoFile, targetType, targetId);
}
```

```dart
import 'package:video_player/video_player.dart';

// Video information extraction
Future<VideoInfo> getVideoInfo(File file) async {
    final controller = VideoPlayerController.file(file);

    try {
        await controller.initialize();

        return VideoInfo(
            duration: controller.value.duration.inMilliseconds,
            width: controller.value.size.width.toInt(),
            height: controller.value.size.height.toInt(),
            size: await file.length(),
        );
    } finally {
        controller.dispose();
    }
}

// Create video post with validation
Future<AmityPost> createValidatedVideoPost(
    String text,
    File videoFile, {
    AmityPostTargetType targetType = AmityPostTargetType.USER,
    String? targetId,
}) async {
    final videoInfo = await getVideoInfo(videoFile);

    const maxSize = 2 * 1024 * 1024 * 1024; // 2GB
    const maxDuration = 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 2 hours in milliseconds

    if (videoInfo.size > maxSize) {
        throw Exception('Video file too large: ${(videoInfo.size / (1024 * 1024)).round()}MB');
    }

    if (videoInfo.duration > maxDuration) {
        throw Exception('Video too long: ${(videoInfo.duration / 60000).round()} minutes');
    }

    return createSingleVideoPost(
        text,
        videoFile,
        targetType: targetType,
        targetId: targetId,
    );
}

class VideoInfo {
    final int duration; // milliseconds
    final int width;
    final int height;
    final int size; // bytes

    VideoInfo({
        required this.duration,
        required this.width,
        required this.height,
        required this.size,
    });
}
```

## Best Practices

    - Compress videos before upload to reduce file size
    - Use appropriate resolution and bitrate for target audience
    - Generate thumbnails for better preview experience
    - Validate video duration and file size before upload

    - Show upload progress for large video files
    - Provide video preview before posting
    - Allow users to trim or edit videos
    - Handle upload failures gracefully with retry options

    - Upload videos in chunks for large files
    - Implement background upload capabilities
    - Cache upload progress to resume interrupted uploads
    - Use appropriate video loading strategies

## Common Use Cases

- **Video Sharing**: Share personal videos and memories
- **Tutorials**: Create step-by-step instructional content
- **Live Updates**: Share real-time event footage
- **Product Demos**: Showcase products in action
- **Entertainment**: Share funny clips and entertainment content
- **Educational Content**: Distribute learning materials and lectures

### [File Posts](https://docs.socialplus.com/social-plus-sdk/social/posts/create-post/file-post)

> Create posts with file attachments like PDFs, documents, and other file formats

**Quick Start:** Upload files first using the [File Handling Guide](/social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/file), then create posts with those file references.

## Overview

File posts allow users to share documents, PDFs, spreadsheets, and other file formats in their social feeds. The social.plus SDK supports up to 10 files per post with a maximum size of 1 GB per file.

### Architecture

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Upload Files] --> B[Get File IDs/Objects]
    B --> C[Create File Post]
    C --> D[Post Published]
```

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `text` | String | Yes | Post content (max 20,000 characters) |
| `files` | Array/IDs | Yes | File objects (iOS/Android/Flutter) or file IDs (TS/JS) |
| `targetType` | Enum | Yes | Target destination (community or user feed) |
| `tags` | Array | No | Searchable tags (not available in Flutter) |
| `metadata` | Object | No | Custom fields for extended functionality |

## File Requirements

- **Maximum files per post:** 10 files
- **Maximum file size:** 1 GB per file
- **Supported formats:** All file types supported by the platform

## Implementation

Use `AmityFilePostBuilder` to create file posts with uploaded file objects.

```swift
// First, upload files using FileRepository
let fileRepository = AmityFileRepository(client: client)
let fileUploader = fileRepository.uploadFile(fileURL: localFileURL) { progress in
    print("Upload progress: \(progress)")
}

fileUploader.done { file in
    // Create file post with uploaded file
    let postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client: client)
    let builder = AmityFilePostBuilder()
        .setText("Check out this document!")
        .setFiles([file])
        .setTargetType(.community)
        .setTargetId("community_id")
        .setTags(["document", "important"])
        .setMetadata(["category": "business"])

    postRepository.createPost(with: builder) { result in
        switch result {
        case .success(let post):
            print("File post created: \(post.postId)")
        case .failure(let error):
            print("Error: \(error.localizedDescription)")
        }
    }
}
```

Use `AmityFilePostCreator.Builder` to create file posts with uploaded file objects.

```kotlin
// First, upload files using FileRepository
val fileRepository = AmityFileRepository(client)
fileRepository.uploadFile(fileUri).subscribe { file ->
    // Create file post with uploaded file
    val postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client)
    val builder = AmityFilePostCreator.Builder()
        .text("Check out this document!")
        .files(listOf(file))
        .targetType(AmityPost.TargetType.COMMUNITY)
        .targetId("community_id")
        .tags(listOf("document", "important"))
        .metadata(mapOf("category" to "business"))

    postRepository.createPost(builder.build()).subscribe(
        { post -> println("File post created: ${post.getPostId()}") },
        { error -> println("Error: ${error.message}") }
    )
}
```

Use file IDs from uploaded files to create file posts.

```typescript
import { PostRepository, FileRepository } from '@amityco/ts-sdk';

// First, upload files and get file IDs
const fileRepository = new FileRepository(client);
const uploadedFile = await fileRepository.createFile({
    file: fileBlob,
    displayName: 'document.pdf'
});

// Create file post with file IDs
const postRepository = new PostRepository(client);
const post = await postRepository.createPost({
    dataType: 'file',
    data: {
        text: 'Check out this document!',
        fileIds: [uploadedFile.fileId]
    },
    targetType: 'community',
    targetId: 'community_id',
    tags: ['document', 'important'],
    metadata: { category: 'business' }
});

console.log('File post created:', post.postId);
```

Use uploaded file objects to create file posts.

```dart

final uploadedFile = await fileRepository.uploadFile(
    File('path/to/document.pdf')
);

// Create file post with uploaded file
final postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client: client);
final builder = AmityFilePostCreator()
    ..text = 'Check out this document!'
    ..files = [uploadedFile]
    ..targetType = AmityPostTargetType.COMMUNITY
    ..targetId = 'community_id'
    ..metadata = {'category': 'business'};

final post = await postRepository.createPost(builder.build());
print('File post created: ${post.postId}');
```

## Error Handling

```swift
postRepository.createPost(with: builder) { result in
    switch result {
    case .success(let post):
        print("Success: \(post.postId)")
    case .failure(let error):
        if let amityError = error as? AmityError {
            switch amityError {
            case .fileTooLarge:
                print("File exceeds size limit")
            case .tooManyFiles:
                print("Too many files (max 10)")
            case .networkError:
                print("Network connection issue")
            default:
                print("Error: \(amityError.localizedDescription)")
            }
        }
    }
}
```

```kotlin
postRepository.createPost(builder.build()).subscribe(
    { post -> println("Success: ${post.getPostId()}") },
    { error ->
        when (error) {
            is AmityException.FileTooLargeException -> 
                println("File exceeds size limit")
            is AmityException.TooManyFilesException -> 
                println("Too many files (max 10)")
            is AmityException.NetworkException -> 
                println("Network connection issue")
            else -> println("Error: ${error.message}")
        }
    }
)
```

```typescript
try {
    const post = await postRepository.createPost(postData);
    console.log('Success:', post.postId);
} catch (error) {
    if (error.code === 'FILE_TOO_LARGE') {
        console.error('File exceeds size limit');
    } else if (error.code === 'TOO_MANY_FILES') {
        console.error('Too many files (max 10)');
    } else if (error.code === 'NETWORK_ERROR') {
        console.error('Network connection issue');
    } else {
        console.error('Error:', error.message);
    }
}
```

```dart
try {
    final post = await postRepository.createPost(builder.build());
    print('Success: ${post.postId}');
} catch (error) {
    if (error is AmityFileTooLargeException) {
        print('File exceeds size limit');
    } else if (error is AmityTooManyFilesException) {
        print('Too many files (max 10)');
    } else if (error is AmityNetworkException) {
        print('Network connection issue');
    } else {
        print('Error: $error');
    }
}
```

## Best Practices

- Keep file sizes reasonable for mobile networks
- Use descriptive filenames
- Consider file type compatibility across platforms
- Implement progress indicators for large uploads

- Show file previews when possible
- Display file sizes and types clearly
- Provide download options
- Handle offline scenarios gracefully

Files cannot be updated once a post is created. Users must create a new post to share updated versions.

### [Custom Posts](https://docs.socialplus.com/social-plus-sdk/social/posts/create-post/custom-post)

> Create custom post types with structured data for specialized content and unique user experiences

**Custom Data Types:** Use the `custom` prefix for all custom post types to ensure proper handling by the SDK.

## Overview

Custom posts allow you to create specialized content types beyond the standard text, image, video, and file posts. Perfect for unique use cases like product catalogs, event listings, recipes, or any structured content that requires custom rendering.

### Key Features

- **Flexible Schema**: Define your own data structure
- **Custom Rendering**: Full control over how content appears in feeds
- **Rich Metadata**: Include complex structured data
- **JSON Payload**: Up to 100KB of custom data per post

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `dataType` | String | Yes | Custom type identifier (must start with "custom") |
| `data` | Object | Yes | Free-form JSON object with your custom structure |
| `text` | String | No | Optional text content |
| `targetType` | Enum | Yes | Target destination (community or user feed) |
| `tags` | Array | No | Searchable tags |
| `metadata` | Object | No | Additional properties |

The `dataType` parameter must have the `custom` prefix (e.g., "custom.recipe", "custom.product").

## Implementation

Create custom posts with structured data using `AmityPostBuilder`.

```swift
// Example: Recipe post
let recipeData: [String: Any] = [
    "title": "Chocolate Chip Cookies",
    "ingredients": [
        ["name": "Flour", "amount": "2 cups"],
        ["name": "Sugar", "amount": "1 cup"],
        ["name": "Chocolate chips", "amount": "1 cup"]
    ],
    "instructions": [
        "Preheat oven to 375°F",
        "Mix dry ingredients",
        "Add chocolate chips",
        "Bake for 10-12 minutes"
    ],
    "prep_time": 15,
    "cook_time": 12,
    "servings": 24,
    "difficulty": "easy",
    "image_url": "https://example.com/cookie.jpg"
]

let postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client: client)
let builder = AmityPostBuilder()
    .setDataType("custom.recipe")
    .setData(recipeData)
    .setText("🍪 Just made these amazing chocolate chip cookies!")
    .setTargetType(.community)
    .setTargetId("cooking_community")
    .setTags(["recipe", "cookies", "baking", "dessert"])
    .setMetadata(["category": "dessert", "featured": true])

postRepository.createPost(with: builder) { result in
    switch result {
    case .success(let post):
        print("Custom recipe post created: \(post.postId)")
    case .failure(let error):
        print("Error: \(error.localizedDescription)")
    }
}
```

Create custom posts with complex data structures.

```kotlin
// Example: Product listing post
val productData = mapOf(
    "title" to "Vintage Camera",
    "price" to 299.99,
    "currency" to "USD",
    "condition" to "excellent",
    "specifications" to mapOf(
        "brand" to "Canon",
        "model" to "AE-1",
        "year" to 1976,
        "lens_included" to true
    ),
    "images" to listOf(
        "https://example.com/camera1.jpg",
        "https://example.com/camera2.jpg"
    ),
    "location" to mapOf(
        "city" to "San Francisco",
        "state" to "CA",
        "country" to "USA"
    ),
    "contact" to mapOf(
        "method" to "message",
        "phone_allowed" to false
    )
)

val postRepository = AmityPostRepository(client)
val builder = AmityPost.PostBuilder()
    .dataType("custom.product")
    .data(productData)
    .text("📷 Beautiful vintage Canon AE-1 for sale! Perfect condition.")
    .targetType(AmityPost.TargetType.COMMUNITY)
    .targetId("marketplace_community")
    .tags(listOf("camera", "vintage", "canon", "photography"))
    .metadata(mapOf("category" to "electronics", "featured" to true))

postRepository.createPost(builder.build()).subscribe(
    { post -> println("Custom product post created: ${post.getPostId()}") },
    { error -> println("Error: ${error.message}") }
```

Create custom posts with TypeScript interfaces for type safety.

```typescript
// Define interfaces for type safety
interface EventData {
    title: string;
    description: string;
    start_date: string;
    end_date: string;
    location: {
        venue: string;
        address: string;
        city: string;
        country: string;
        coordinates?: {
            lat: number;
            lng: number;
        };
    };
    organizer: {
        name: string;
        contact: string;
    };
    ticket_info: {
        price: number;
        currency: string;
        available_tickets: number;
        registration_url?: string;
    };
    categories: string[];
}

// Example: Event listing post
const eventData: EventData = {
    title: "Web Development Conference 2024",
    description: "Join industry leaders for talks on latest web technologies",
    start_date: "2024-06-15T09:00:00Z",
    end_date: "2024-06-15T17:00:00Z",
    location: {
        venue: "Tech Convention Center",
        address: "123 Innovation Drive",
        city: "San Francisco",
        country: "USA",
        coordinates: {
            lat: 37.7749,
            lng: -122.4194
        }
    },
    organizer: {
        name: "Tech Events Inc",
        contact: "info@techevents.com"
    },
    ticket_info: {
        price: 199,
        currency: "USD",
        available_tickets: 500,
        registration_url: "https://register.techevents.com"
    },
    categories: ["conference", "web-development", "technology"]
};

const postRepository = new PostRepository(client);
const post = await postRepository.createPost({
    dataType: 'custom.event',
    data: eventData,
    text: '🎯 Don\'t miss the biggest web development conference of the year!',
    targetType: 'community',
    targetId: 'tech_events_community',
    tags: ['conference', 'web-dev', 'networking', '2024'],
    metadata: { featured: true, early_bird: false }
});

console.log('Custom event post created:', post.postId);
```
